channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: Hurricane Milton has caused thousands of flight cancellations, is yours one of them? What to know
item.description: Thousands of flights in and out of the U.S. have been canceled this week as Hurricane Milton barreled into the Gulf of Mexico and plowed across Florida.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham-Philips And David Koenig, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta AI launches in Brazil, U.K., and more markets in a bid to beat ChatGPT
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Wednesday its artificial intelligence chatbot, Meta AI, will be available in 21 new markets, as it looks to compete with OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Why it’s important to support fintechs in the acceleration age
item.description: In a time where the digital world is shifting beneath our feet, the speed of technological change can feel like it has never been greater. Tech advancements achieve widespread adoption seemingly at breakneck speeds.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Colella, Fast Company

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Big shift in IT employment shows new skills are needed
item.description: Multiple research firms reported that the unemployment rate for IT workers dropped precipitously last month — in one instance, by nearly 37% — a decline that marked a serious shift from months of incremental increases mixed with plateaus in unemployment.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft brings Copilot AI tools to OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot is now available in OneDrive, part of a wider revamp of the company’s cloud storage platform. Microsoft highlighted several ways to use the generative AI (genAI) assistant in OneDrive in a blog post Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Toyota Invests $500 Million in Joby Aviation for Air Mobility Vision
item.description: Toyota's investment in Joby Aviation aims to certify and commercialize electric air taxis, revolutionizing air mobility with sustainability and speed.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ suggests splitting off Chrome and Android to break Google’s monopoly
item.description: In a significant escalation of its antitrust battle with Google, the US Department of Justice (DOJ) has proposed splitting off Google’s Chrome browser and Android operating system as part of sweeping remedies aimed at curbing the tech giant’s “illegal monopoly” in online search and advertising.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Electron vs. Tauri: Which cross-platform framework is for you?
item.description: If there’s one thing developers agree on about cross-platform applications, it’s that it’s easier to create a GUI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript than to use one of the many cross-platform frameworks out there. Frameworks like Electron give you the best of both worlds.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Inflection AI Shifts Enterprise Platform to Intel's Gaudi 3 Accelerators
item.description:
Inflection AI shifts its latest enterprise platform to Intel's Gaudi 3 accelerators, stepping away from Nvidia GPUs. The move showcases Intel Gaudi 3 as a viable alternative to Nvidia and highlights the growing interest in Intel's AI hardware across the industry.")

Inflection AI Shifts Enterprise Platform to Intel's Gaudi 3 Accelerators

The technology world is abuzz with news that Inflection AI, a pioneering firm in conversational AI, has shifted its latest enterprise platform to Intel's Gaudi 3 accelerators. This move signifies a significant departure from Nvidia GPUs, which have long dominated the AI chip market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ask Kurt: How does Apple AirTag work, how far can you track?
item.description: Can you use an AirTag to track a pet? Is it worth it? Here’s how far it will track. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI-generated fake nudes now part of Russian cybercrime group's method
item.description: Cybercrime group Fin7 has created at least seven websites serving malware through generators that create fake nude images.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Is the stock market in an AI bubble?
item.description: When it comes to AI investments, “a lot of money is going to get wasted,” MIT professor Daron Acemoglu told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11
